headline: Choose the buying lane that matches how you want to move
subheadline: VoiceToWebsite closes best when software buyers can check out fast, implementation buyers can submit a strong brief, and enterprise buyers can qualify scope before commitment.
stepsHeadline: Use pricing to route yourself into the right next step

steps.title: Buy Operator OS
steps.description: Choose this lane if you already know the product fits and you want the direct software offer.

steps.title: Submit Launch Sprint
steps.description: Choose this lane if you want guidance, setup, and faster activation before you scale self-serve usage.

steps.title: Qualify Enterprise
steps.description: Choose this lane if governance, hosting, rollout, or custom scope needs review before commercial commitment.

tiers.name: Starter
tiers.description: A lightweight entry point for exploring the platform, planning workflows, and validating fit.

tiers.name: Operator
tiers.description: The core software offer for founders and small teams running marketing, delivery, and internal operations.

tiers.name: Launch Sprint
tiers.description: A done-with-you setup package for businesses that want a fast implementation instead of a blank dashboard.
features: A faster path to first revenue outcome

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.description: For teams that need self-hosting, governance, onboarding, and deeper operational support.

sections.title: Operator OS is the checkout-first offer
sections.description: This lane is optimized for recurring software revenue and should be the fastest path from conviction to payment.

sections.title: Launch Sprint is the lead-form-first offer
sections.description: This lane is optimized for buyers who need help before they are ready to operate the platform on their own.

sections.title: Enterprise is the review-first offer
sections.description: This lane is optimized for buyers who care about governance, hosting, approvals, and rollout certainty.

faq.question: Why mix subscriptions and services?
faq.answer: Because services create early cash flow and implementation success, while subscriptions create recurring revenue and scale over time.

faq.question: Should the free plan be generous?
faq.answer: It should be useful enough to create trust, but limited enough that serious buyers naturally move into Operator or Launch Sprint.

cta.heading: Use pricing to choose between checkout, implementation, and qualification.
cta.body: The page should help buyers commit faster because each lane is matched to a real buying style instead of forcing every visitor into the same close path.
